title: Wireless LAN Controller Working Process
subtitle: How APs, WLCs, and Wireless Clients interact inside an enterprise WLAN.

steps.title: Access Point Boots
steps.desc: The Access Point powers on using PoE and obtains an IP address from DHCP.
details: AP receives power through PoE-enabled switch
Hardware initialization process begins
AP loads operating system and firmware
DHCP request is sent to obtain an IP address
Network parameters such as gateway and DNS are assigned
AP becomes ready for controller discovery

steps.title: WLC Discovery
steps.desc: The AP discovers the Wireless LAN Controller using DHCP Option 43, DNS, or broadcast discovery.
details: AP searches for available Wireless LAN Controllers
DHCP Option 43 may provide controller IP addresses
DNS lookup for CISCO-CAPWAP-CONTROLLER can be used
Layer 2 broadcast discovery may occur on local networks
Previously configured controller information can be used
Best available controller is selected

steps.title: CAPWAP Join
steps.desc: The AP establishes a secure CAPWAP tunnel and joins the controller.
details: AP initiates CAPWAP control connection
Certificate validation is performed
Secure CAPWAP tunnel is established
Controller downloads configuration to AP
Firmware compatibility is verified
AP successfully joins and registers with the WLC

steps.title: SSID Broadcast
steps.desc: The AP starts advertising configured WLANs and SSIDs to nearby clients.
details: Controller pushes WLAN configuration to AP
SSID beacon frames begin transmission
Wireless clients can detect available networks
Security and VLAN policies are applied
Radio parameters become active
Coverage becomes available for users

steps.title: Client Authentication
steps.desc: Wireless clients authenticate using PSK, WPA2/WPA3 Enterprise, or 802.1X.
details: Client selects and joins the SSID
Authentication process begins
PSK or Enterprise credentials are validated
RADIUS server may participate in authentication
Authorization policies are applied
Client receives network access permissions

steps.title: Traffic Forwarding
steps.desc: User traffic is forwarded through the AP and controller according to WLAN policies.
details: Client traffic enters the AP
Traffic is encapsulated through CAPWAP when required
Controller applies QoS and security policies
Traffic is forwarded to the destination network
Monitoring and analytics continue in real time
Users access enterprise resources and internet services
